content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: My birthday is in ___, which is the second month of the year.
distractor_rationale: January is the first month, not the second — contradicts 'second month'
October is the tenth month, not the second — contradicts 'second month'
June is the sixth month, not the second — contradicts 'second month'

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The school week starts on ___, and most children go back to class after the weekend.
distractor_rationale: Thursday is the fourth weekday, not the first day after the weekend — contradicts 'starts'
afternoon is a time of day, not a day of the week — wrong category for a school-week start
evening is a time of day, not a day of the week — wrong category entirely

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: January is the first month of the year, so the ___ after it is February.
distractor_rationale: day is a 24-hour period, not a calendar unit that follows a month — wrong scale
evening is a part of the day, not a calendar unit — wrong category
century is 100 years, far too large a unit to follow a single month — contradicts the context

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: After dinner, Tom usually reads a book in the ___ before going to bed at around ten o'clock.
distractor_rationale: afternoon ends before dinner for most people, so reading after dinner cannot be in the afternoon — contradicts 'after dinner'
autumn is a season, not a time of day — wrong category for filling the gap after 'in the'
morning comes before dinner, not after it — contradicts 'after dinner'

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The leaves on the trees turn red and orange in ___, just before winter arrives.
distractor_rationale: June is a summer month when leaves are green and full — contradicts 'before winter'
January is a winter month, not the season before winter — contradicts 'just before winter'
February is also a winter month, not the season that precedes winter — contradicts 'just before winter'

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Could you please check the ___ on the calendar? We need to know exactly when the meeting is scheduled.
distractor_rationale: degree measures temperature or angles, not a position on a calendar — wrong meaning
century refers to a 100-year period, far too broad for scheduling a meeting — contradicts 'exactly when'
afternoon is a time of day but cannot be 'checked on a calendar' to confirm a specific scheduled event — wrong collocation

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The weather forecast says it will be ___ all week, with no rain expected until the weekend.
distractor_rationale: freeze is a verb or noun describing extreme cold, not an adjective describing a week of no rain — wrong part of speech and meaning
evening is a time of day, not a weather condition — wrong category
October is a month, not a weather adjective — wrong category entirely

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Temperatures can ___ overnight in the mountains in winter, so always bring extra warm clothing.
distractor_rationale: dry describes a lack of moisture and cannot collocate with 'overnight temperatures' as a verb — wrong meaning and collocation
degree is a noun measuring temperature, not a verb describing what temperatures do overnight — wrong part of speech
date is a noun referring to a calendar point and cannot function as a verb meaning to drop below zero — wrong meaning

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The thermometer showed only three ___ above zero when Sara left for work early on Thursday morning.
distractor_rationale: days are units of time and cannot be measured on a thermometer — contradicts 'thermometer showed'
dates are calendar points and are not measured on a thermometer — contradicts 'thermometer showed'
centuries are 100-year periods and cannot appear on a thermometer reading — contradicts 'thermometer showed'

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Historians believe the printing press changed communication so dramatically that its effects lasted for more than a ___.
distractor_rationale: afternoon is a few hours long, far too short a period for lasting historical effects — contradicts 'more than a'
Monday is a single day of the week, not a long historical time span — contradicts the scale implied by 'historians' and 'dramatically'
date is a specific point in time, not a duration — cannot follow 'more than a' to express a long period

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: My birthday is in ___, so we always celebrate with a big cake in the middle of winter.
distractor_rationale: June is a summer month, which contradicts 'middle of winter' stated in the sentence
October is an autumn month, not the middle of winter
August is a summer month, which directly contradicts 'middle of winter'

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The children play outside every ___ after school, before it gets dark at six o'clock.
distractor_rationale: morning is before school, not after school finishes
evening begins after it gets dark, but the sentence says they play before it gets dark at six
midnight is the middle of the night and children would not play outside then

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The leaves on the trees turn red and orange every ___, just before the cold weather arrives in November.
distractor_rationale: spring is when new leaves grow and appear green, not when they turn red and orange before November
century is a period of one hundred years and cannot describe a seasonal change in nature
Monday is a day of the week and cannot describe a season when leaves change colour

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The pipes in the old building began to ___ during the night because the temperature dropped to minus ten degrees.
distractor_rationale: date means to write or note a date on something and cannot describe what pipes do in extreme cold
dry means to remove moisture, which is the opposite of what happens when water turns to ice in cold temperatures
degree is a unit of measurement and cannot function as a verb describing what happens to pipes in cold weather

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The project manager asked everyone to confirm the exact ___ of the meeting so she could send the official invitations with the correct details.
distractor_rationale: century refers to a hundred-year period and cannot be confirmed for a specific meeting in the near future
degree is a unit of temperature or academic qualification and cannot be confirmed as a calendar detail for sending invitations
afternoon describes a general part of the day but cannot serve as the specific calendar detail needed for an official invitation, which requires a full date

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: My birthday is in ___, the second month of the year.
distractor_rationale: October is the tenth month, not the second month of the year
June is the sixth month, not the second month of the year
Monday is a day of the week, not a month at all

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The school week starts on ___, so I always set my alarm early on that day.
distractor_rationale: Thursday is the fourth weekday, not the first day of the school week
January is a month, not a day of the week
afternoon is a part of the day, not a day of the week

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The sun sets early in ___, when the leaves turn red and orange and fall from the trees.
distractor_rationale: a century is a period of one hundred years and has no connection to leaves falling
a date is a specific point in time, not a season when leaves turn and fall
a degree is a unit of temperature or angle, not a season of the year

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: We usually have dinner together as a family in the ___, after everyone comes home from work and school.
distractor_rationale: afternoon refers to the period before 6 p.m., which is before most people return home from work
January is a month of the year, not a time of day
a century is a period of one hundred years, not a time of day

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The weather in ___ is very hot and sunny, which makes it a popular month for beach holidays.
distractor_rationale: October is an autumn month associated with cooler temperatures, not a peak beach holiday month
February is a winter month with cold weather, contradicting the hot and sunny description
Thursday is a day of the week, not a month of the year

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The pipes in the old house burst because the water inside them started to ___ during the cold winter night.
distractor_rationale: dry means to lose moisture, which does not cause pipes to burst in cold weather
date means to record a time or to go out with someone, not a process that happens to water in cold temperatures
degree is a unit of measurement and cannot describe what water does to cause pipes to burst

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Please write the ___ on the form — the office needs to know the exact day, month, and year you signed it.
distractor_rationale: day alone gives only the name such as Monday and does not include the month and year as required
afternoon is a time of day, not a combination of day, month, and year
degree is a unit of temperature or angle and cannot be written on a form to show when it was signed

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The temperature dropped to minus five ___ overnight, so the roads were covered in ice by morning.
distractor_rationale: dates refers to specific points in time on a calendar and cannot follow a temperature number
days is a unit of time and cannot be used to measure temperature
centuries are periods of one hundred years and have no connection to measuring temperature

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The region had almost no rain for three months, and the farmers were worried because the soil had become completely ___.
distractor_rationale: freeze is a verb describing a process and cannot function as an adjective to describe the condition of soil
autumn is a season of the year and cannot describe the physical condition of soil
January is a month of the year and cannot be used as an adjective to describe soil

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The ancient temple was built more than two ___ ago, meaning it has stood for over two hundred years.
distractor_rationale: afternoons are parts of a day and cannot represent a span of one hundred years each
dates are specific points on a calendar and cannot be counted to represent periods of one hundred years
degrees are units of temperature or angle and cannot represent periods of one hundred years

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: We have a short ___ break at three o'clock every day. (AFTERNOON)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The weather in ___ is usually very cold and snowy in this region. (FREEZE)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The summer months are generally ___, with almost no rain from June to August. (DRY)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The leaves turn red and orange during the ___ months, especially in October and November. (AUTUMN)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Historians believe that the building was constructed in the early part of the nineteenth ___, sometime between 1810 and 1830. (CENTURY)
